strengths: Practical ETL and data modeling experience
Quantified impact on KPIs and automation
weaknesses: No clear multi-year Data Science title for 4+ years
Short tenure in formal data science roles
improvement_bullets: Integrated distributed data systems by migrating legacy data to AWS and Snowflake, utilizing Python for Spark-based data processing to optimize large-scale analytics.
Designed and implemented A/B testing frameworks to evaluate product features, measuring impact on user retention and conversion to drive evidence-based product iteration.
Partnered with product and finance teams to develop internal analytics platforms, building dashboards and automated reporting systems that democratized data access for non-technical stakeholders.
Engineered scalable data pipelines utilizing distributed computing patterns, ensuring high data quality and reliability for enterprise-wide decision-making frameworks.
Collaborated with cross-functional stakeholders to conduct complex product analytics, utilizing time-series forecasting to predict user growth trends and inform product roadmap strategy.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Scientist opportunity with Figma. I am interested in this opportunity because of Figma's commitment to making design accessible through powerful, real-time collaboration tools. What interests me about working for Figma is its unique approach to empowering teams to move from idea to product, which inspires me because I am passionate about building the data infrastructure that supports creative workflows. I believe my technical fluency in SQL and Python and my experience in cross-functional data modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on data-driven efficiency. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified that manual reporting was a bottleneck for our executive team. I designed and deployed interactive Power BI dashboards that automated the entire pipeline, which improved KPI visibility and reduced data processing time by 40%. Ultimately, I know that the Data Scientist role at Figma would allow me to bring my expertise in data modeling to streamline the complex analytical requirements of your product and finance teams. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst and Operations Lead enabled me to develop my A/B testing and experimentation skills. I utilized time-series modeling to identify high-value customer segments, which allowed us to launch targeted marketing initiatives that drove a 300% increase in new customer acquisition over 12 months. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in distributed data systems and scalable ETL pipelines in working on product-led growth at Figma. I know that my analytical rigor and collaborative mindset align with what it takes to succeed in the Data Scientist role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
